📋 Terms of Use
Verified Peptides Network — Please read and agree to continue
1. RESEARCH USE ONLY
This Platform is a private, community-operated research reference database. All compounds, data, and content are for laboratory and scientific research purposes only. Nothing herein constitutes medical advice, treatment, or a recommendation for human use of any substance.

2. NO ENDORSEMENT OF HUMAN CONSUMPTION
VPN does not advocate, endorse, or condone the administration of any research chemical to any human being. Users who engage with any compound outside of an authorized research setting do so entirely at their own risk and assume full legal and medical responsibility.

3. PUBLIC SAFETY PURPOSE
This database exists to protect researchers and the public by providing transparent, third-party verified COA, endotoxin, and heavy metal testing data on research compounds sold by wholesale vendors. It is a community safety tool, not a commercial service.

4. USER ELIGIBILITY
You confirm you are 18 years of age or older and that you access this Platform solely for lawful research or educational purposes in compliance with all laws applicable in your jurisdiction.

5. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
VPN, its administrators, and contributors shall not be liable for any damages arising from use of or reliance on any content on this Platform. Vendor listings do not constitute endorsement of any vendor's products for any purpose.

6. DATA INTEGRITY
All COA data is sourced from accredited third-party labs. VPN makes no warranty of completeness or accuracy. Lab results reflect specific batches tested at the time of testing only.

Research Purposes Only — Not For Human Consumption
All peptides and compounds referenced on this platform are research chemicals only. This database does not advocate, encourage, or endorse human use of any compound listed. COA Uploads Are Admin-Restricted
To protect the integrity of this database, only authorized admins can upload COA, endotoxin, and heavy metal test results. This ensures nothing is submitted that could compromise the group or the vendors we trust.

⚠
Not Medical Advice — Tools I Use for My Own Health
Everything on this page is for informational and educational purposes only. These are tools and resources I personally use to better understand my own health. Nothing here constitutes medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always consult a qualified medical professional before making any health decisions.
My Personal Protocol — How I Did It
I started by going to my doctor with the comprehensive letter templates below — these are full panels covering metabolic health, insulin resistance, hormones, inflammation, fatty liver, and more. That first full panel showed me exactly what I was dealing with. I did my research based on those results, made changes, and started my protocols.

After that foundation was set, I use Rythm Health every 1–3 months to track whether I'm progressing or declining — without needing a doctor visit every time. It's not a replacement for the full panel, it's a monitoring tool for after you already know your baseline.
STEP 1 — START HERE
🩺
Full Panel With Your Doctor
The doctor letter templates below are a comprehensive blood panel — covering metabolic health, insulin resistance, inflammation, hormones, thyroid, fatty liver, and more. This is your starting point. You need to know your baseline before anything else.
See letter templates below ↓
STEP 2 — ONGOING MONITORING
📈
Rythm Health — Track Progress
Not a replacement for the full panel — this is what I use every 1–3 months after my baseline is established. At-home testing to see if I'm progressing or declining based on my protocols. No doctor visit needed.

DOCTOR LETTER TEMPLATES
How to Send This to Your Doctor
1 Download the image for your gender below (long press on mobile or right-click → Save Image)
2 Upload it to ChatGPT or Grok and type: "Extract the text from this image"
3 Copy the extracted text, add your name at the top, and email it to your doctor requesting the listed tests
✓ I do this annually or bi-annually to rule out insulin resistance, metabolic syndrome, inflammation, fatty liver, and hormonal imbalances

Terms of Use & Legal Disclaimer
Verified Peptides Network (VPN) · Effective Date: 2026 · Last Updated: May 2026
IMPORTANT — READ BEFORE USING THIS PLATFORM
By accessing or using the Verified Peptides Network ("VPN," "the Platform," "we," "us"), you ("User") acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms of Use in their entirety. If you do not agree, you must immediately discontinue use of this Platform. This Platform is a private, community-operated research reference database. It is not a commercial entity, medical provider, pharmacy, or regulatory body.
1. PURPOSE & SCOPE OF THE PLATFORM
1.1 The Verified Peptides Network is a community-operated, independent research reference database dedicated solely to documenting and sharing third-party laboratory testing results — including Certificates of Analysis (COA), endotoxin tests, and heavy metal screening — conducted on peptide compounds sourced from wholesale research vendors.

1.2 The express purpose of this Platform is to protect the research community and the general public by providing transparent, independently verified data on compound purity, identity, and contamination levels. This serves as a public safety resource to reduce the risk of harm from untested or misrepresented research chemicals.

1.3 All compounds referenced are classified as research chemicals only. They are sold and used exclusively for in-vitro laboratory research, preclinical animal studies, and scientific investigation. They are not intended, approved, or marketed for human therapeutic use, human consumption, or veterinary administration unless specifically authorized by the applicable regulatory authority in the User's jurisdiction.
2. NO MEDICAL ADVICE — NO ENDORSEMENT OF HUMAN USE
2.1 Nothing on this Platform constitutes medical advice, medical diagnosis, treatment recommendations, or a prescription. No content should be interpreted as a suggestion, promotion, or encouragement of human use of any compound listed.

2.2 VPN does not advocate, endorse, facilitate, or condone the administration of any research compound to any human being. All dosing information, reconstitution calculations, peptide profiles, and protocol references are provided exclusively as scientific and laboratory reference material.

2.3 Users who choose to engage with any compound outside of an authorized laboratory or clinical research setting do so entirely at their own risk and assume full legal and medical responsibility. VPN expressly disclaims any responsibility for any such use.
3. INDEPENDENT TESTING & COA DATA INTEGRITY
3.1 All COA, endotoxin, and heavy metal test results published on this Platform are sourced from accredited third-party laboratories. VPN does not conduct laboratory testing itself.

3.2 While every effort is made to ensure accuracy, VPN makes no warranty, express or implied, regarding the completeness, accuracy, or fitness for any particular purpose of any data published herein. Laboratory results reflect the specific batch and lot tested at the time of testing and may not be representative of all batches from any vendor.

3.3 COA data upload access is restricted to authorized administrators only to preserve database integrity. Unauthorized attempts to modify, falsify, or corrupt data on this Platform may constitute a violation of applicable computer fraud and cybercrime statutes.
4. USER ELIGIBILITY & RESPONSIBILITIES
4.1 By using this Platform, you represent and warrant that you are at least 18 years of age and that you access this Platform solely for lawful research, educational, or scientific reference purposes.

4.2 You agree not to use this Platform to: (a) facilitate the purchase, sale, or distribution of controlled substances; (b) misrepresent the nature or intended use of any research compound; (c) engage in any activity that violates applicable federal, state, or local laws or regulations in your jurisdiction.

4.3 Users are solely responsible for ensuring that their access to and use of any information on this Platform complies with all laws applicable in their jurisdiction. Laws governing research chemicals vary significantly by country and region.
5. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
5.1 TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, VPN, its administrators, contributors, and affiliated community members shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, punitive, or special damages arising from or related to your use of or reliance on any content, data, or information available on this Platform.

5.2 VPN is not liable for the actions, products, or representations of any vendor listed on this Platform. Vendor listings do not constitute endorsement, recommendation, or certification of any vendor's products for any purpose.

5.3 You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless VPN and its administrators from any claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, or expenses (including legal fees) arising from your violation of these Terms or your use of this Platform.
6. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY & DATA SUBMISSIONS
6.1 All original content, design, and compilation of data on this Platform is the intellectual property of VPN and its contributors. Unauthorized reproduction, distribution, or commercial use is prohibited.

6.2 By submitting COA data or testing results to VPN, contributors grant VPN a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to publish, display, and distribute such data for community research and public safety purposes. Contributors warrant that submitted data is authentic and obtained from accredited laboratories.
7. PRIVACY & COMMUNITY CONDUCT
7.1 VPN collects no personal data from general users. Access credentials are managed internally and not shared with third parties.

7.2 Community members are expected to conduct themselves with integrity. Any attempt to sabotage, misrepresent, or weaponize this Platform against vendors, researchers, or community members in bad faith is a violation of these Terms and may result in access revocation and referral to appropriate authorities.
8. GOVERNING LAW & DISPUTE RESOLUTION
8.1 These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the United States. Any disputes arising from these Terms or use of this Platform shall be resolved through binding arbitration or in the appropriate courts of competent jurisdiction.

8.2 VPN reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time. Continued use of the Platform following any modification constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.
ACKNOWLEDGMENT
By accessing this Platform you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to these Terms of Use in full. You acknowledge that this Platform exists to promote transparency, protect public safety, and advance legitimate scientific research — and you agree to use it exclusively for those lawful purposes.
Questions or concerns: vpnpepdata@yahoo.com
About VPN Database
What is this?

The Verified Peptides Network COA Database is a centralized repository of certificates of authenticity, endotoxin test results, and heavy metal analysis for trusted wholesale peptide vendors. All reports are third-party verified.

Test Types
COACertificate of Authenticity — purity and identity confirmation
EndotoxinLAL/rFC test — bacterial contamination screening
Heavy MetalsICP-MS analysis — lead, arsenic, cadmium, mercury limits

VPN
⚡ Connect Database
Uploads save in memory only.
Connect Supabase to persist reports.
See setup guide below.
Database Setup
Step 1 — Create Free Supabase Account
1. Go to supabase.com → Sign up free
2. Create a new project (name it anything)
3. Wait ~2 minutes for it to provision
Step 2 — Create the Reports Table
In Supabase → SQL Editor → paste and run this:
create table reports ( id uuid default gen_random_uuid() primary key, created_at timestamptz default now(), vendor_id text, peptide text, test_type text, file_name text, file_data text, lab text, batch text, notes text ); alter table reports enable row level security; create policy "Public read" on reports for select using (true); create policy "Public insert" on reports for insert with check (true);
Step 3 — Get Your Keys
In Supabase → Project Settings → API:
Copy your Project URL
Copy your anon public key
Step 4 — Paste Keys into the HTML File
Open your index.html in a text editor
Find these two lines near the top of the script:

const SUPA_URL = '';
const SUPA_KEY = '';

Paste your URL and key between the quotes, save, and re-upload to Netlify.
✓ Free tier: 500MB storage, unlimited rows
✓ Reports survive refreshes and persist forever
✓ All vendors share the same database
✓ Takes about 5 minutes total
